commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The protected label and upgrade/security-sensitive fallback semantics need maintainer handling rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.; Cleared: No concrete security regression was found; the new ClawHub artifact-miss fallback is gated to parsed official @openclaw/* npm specs and keeps existing install validation paths. (sha=ac4999e1553b7221da2031269a682f7302c70c9c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes CLI update/doctor package handoff metadata, managed service package-root targeting, and ClawHub-to-official-npm fallback behavior with focused tests and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level contracts: current main still has the old parent-supported deferral behavior and lacks artifact-unavailable fallback handling. I did not establish a live package-update reproduction path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The protected label and upgrade/security-sensitive fallback semantics need maintainer handling rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: fix(update): harden legacy package handoff
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep legacy package-update doctor repairs old-parent-readable by preserving source config metadata while still allowing modern parents to defer plugin package repair explicitly.
- Fall back to npm only for official `@openclaw/*` npm plugin packages when ClawHub package metadata exists but the ClawPack artifact download is unavailable.
- Target the managed gateway service package root during package updates and honor direct `node_modules` roots without breaking ordinary npm bin shim refreshes.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/update-phase.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/infra/update-global.test.ts src/infra/package-update-steps.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` (9 files, 322 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/npm-registry-spec.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` (4 files, 206 tests)
- `pnpm openclaw update --dry-run --json --channel stable` (live CLI dry-run; no config writes)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none` (clean on `ac4999e1553b7221da2031269a682f7302c70c9c`)
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:changed` (Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01krwf9bgences8s6jt9zv0zvd`, https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26010618814)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: legacy package updates from an old parent can run candidate doctor repairs without future-stamping config metadata; ClawHub artifact 404s fall back to npm only when the target npm package is an official `@openclaw/*` plugin package; package updates target the managed service package root instead of the invoking checkout/root.
Real environment tested: local focused Vitest for update, doctor, ClawHub, package-target, and official-only fallback contracts; live OpenClaw CLI dry-run in the repo checkout; Blacksmith Testbox changed core/coreTests/docs lanes.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw update --dry-run --json --channel stable`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/npm-registry-spec.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --reviewer codex --fallback-reviewer none`; `git diff --check`; `pnpm check:changed` via Testbox `tbx_01krwf9bgences8s6jt9zv0zvd`.
Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 4 files and 206 tests; branch autoreview returned no accepted/actionable findings; Testbox `tbx_01krwf9bgences8s6jt9zv0zvd` exited 0; live CLI dry-run produced this terminal capture:

```console
$ pnpm openclaw update --dry-run --json --channel stable
{
  "dryRun": true,
  "root": "/Users/steipete/Projects/clawdbot6",
  "installKind": "git",
  "mode": "npm",
  "updateInstallKind": "package",
  "switchToGit": false,
  "switchToPackage": true,
  "restart": true,
  "requestedChannel": "stable",
  "storedChannel": "beta",
  "effectiveChannel": "stable",
  "tag": "openclaw@latest",
  "targetVersion": "2026.5.12",
  "downgradeRisk": false,
  "actions": [
    "Persist update.channel=stable in config",
    "Switch install mode from git to package manager (npm)",
    "Run plugin update sync after core update",
    "Refresh shell completion cache (if needed)",
    "Restart gateway service and run doctor checks"
  ],
  "notes": []
}
```

Observed result after fix: changed lanes passed, legacy metadata override is covered, direct npm node_modules swapping is covered, ordinary npm shim refresh remains covered, official `@openclaw/*` ClawHub-to-npm fallback is covered, non-OpenClaw npm fallback is blocked in doctor, onboarding, and update bridge paths, and the live CLI dry-run completed without writing config.
What was not tested: full Package Acceptance published-upgrade-survivor lane from beta.4 to this PR package was not run.
target.author: steipete
